text.title: Use Case Modeling — Examples
text.summary: Place Order UC example with main/alternative/exception flows; common antipatterns.

example.note: UC-101: Place Order. Primary Actor: Customer (human). System: E-Commerce Platform.
Preconditions: Customer is logged in; shopping cart has at least one item.

Main Flow (5 steps): 1. Customer clicks Checkout — system displays order summary. 2. Customer confirms shipping address — system calculates shipping cost. 3. Customer selects payment method — system displays payment form. 4. Customer enters payment details — system validates payment. 5. Customer clicks Place Order — system creates order and sends confirmation email.
Alternative Flow AF-1 (New Shipping Address): At step 2, customer enters a new address — system validates and saves — returns to step 2.
Exception Flow EX-1 (Payment Declined): At step 4, payment validation fails — system displays error message — customer can retry or select different payment method.
Postconditions (success): order created with status Confirmed; inventory reduced; confirmation email sent.

antipattern.description: Feature as use case: "Display Product Screen" or "Show Report".
antipattern.reason: These describe system behavior, not actor goals. A use case must capture what the actor achieves, not what the system renders.

antipattern.description: Missing secondary actors: e.g., no Payment Gateway actor on a payment UC.
antipattern.reason: External systems that participate in the interaction belong in the diagram; omitting them produces an incomplete system boundary.

antipattern.description: No exception flows — only happy path documented.
antipattern.reason: Exceptions are where the system fails and users abandon. A UC without exception flows cannot drive error-handling design or negative test coverage.

antipattern.description: UI details in system response: "system highlights the Submit button" or "system shows a green popup".
antipattern.reason: UI elements change across design iterations; system responses must use behavior verbs (validates, persists, emits, notifies) to remain stable across redesigns.

antipattern.description: Too abstract: "Use System" as a use case name.
antipattern.reason: UC names must encode a specific actor goal. Abstract names cannot drive test scaffolding or traceability.

antipattern.description: More than 9 steps in main flow without splitting.
antipattern.reason: Oversized main flows bundle multiple actor goals; they mislead estimation and test scaffolding. Trigger a split when step count exceeds 9.
